response.justification: While the candidate has extensive technical development experience and some exposure to developer tools infrastructure (CI/CD, Docker, MCP frameworks), the resume shows zero evidence of the required 2+ years of formal people management experience. This is a critical gap for a "Full-time people-leadership" management role. Teaching workshops and mentoring through open source do not substitute for direct reports, hiring, performance management, and team development responsibilities.
strengths: 34 years of software development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems—substantially exceeds minimum requirements
Demonstrated developer tools and infrastructure knowledge through CI/CD systems, GitLab administration, Docker, and recent MCP code generator work
Proven ability to develop others through workshops, courses (Selenium, Web Internals), and organizing team coding practices (Refactoring Dojos)
concerns: No evidence of formal people management experience; resume contains no mention of direct reports, hiring, performance reviews, or team leadership responsibilities
Developer tools expertise is scattered across career and not primary focus; no clear deep specialization in build systems, testing infrastructure, or release automation at scale
Limited evidence of large-scale mobile platform ecosystem experience or strategic organizational leadership; mobile work was at smaller companies
